disregard_when: the synthetic trap is unrealistic or too leading
the constructed expected label is not defensible
the Gemini output is missing or malformed
the deterministic policy already handles the class and no new rule is needed

market.description: Resolves Yes if a Gemini-branded model is ranked first on the public Chatbot Arena leaderboard at market close on December 31, 2026.
market.resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if a Gemini-branded model is ranked first on the public Chatbot Arena leaderboard at market close on December 31, 2026.
market.title: Will Gemini hold the #1 Chatbot Arena spot on Dec 31, 2026?

model_fit_proposal_json: {"advisory_inverse_market_check": {"candidate_market_ids": [], "could_be_useful": false, "rationale": "The Yes outcome of the market aligns with the positive stance of the thesis (Gemini improving/succeeding), so an inverse check is not necessary.", "supporting_outcome": "n/a"}, "captures": ["Gemini's performance relative to frontier models in 2026", "Leaderboard ranking comparison"], "fit_reason": "The market resolves Gemini's relative performance ranking on a major leaderboard (Chatbot Arena) at the end of 2026, which is a central milestone for assessing whether Gemini has closed the performance gap with frontier models. However, it is indirect because holding the #1 spot is a much stricter condition than simply closing the gap, and the market does not capture or verify the causal role of Google's TPU progress.", "misses": ["The causal link to Google TPU progress", "Scenarios where Gemini significantly closes the gap but remains ranked below #1"], "recommended_market_id": "synth_gemini_arena_2026", "rejected_markets": [], "semantic_fit_class": "indirect"}
policy_gap_summary: Gemini advisory classification disagreed with a synthetic expected label. Human review should decide whether this is a real policy blind spot, a prompt issue, or synthetic noise to disregard.
source_text: Google's TPU v8 infrastructure means Gemini can train 3x faster, closing the frontier model gap.
